bibliographic_info.title: Hong Huang1,2 · Wang Gao1,2 · Shuguo Pan1,2 · Longlei Qiao1,2 · Qing Zhao1,2

grounding_quotes: <!-- PAGE: 1 -->  GPS Solutions (2026) 30:30 https://doi.org/10.1007/s10291-025-01998-3 RESEARCH Asynchronous RTK positioning enhanced by satellite-based PPP corrections: a case study with PPP-B2b Hong Huang1,2 · Wang Gao1,2 · Shuguo Pan1,2 · Longlei Qiao1,2 · Qing Zhao1,2 Received: 17 June 2025 / Accepted: 12 November 2025 / Published online: 22 November 2025 © The Author(s), under exclusive lice
To address this issue, this paper proposes an asynchronous RTK method enhanced by satellite-based PPP corrections, with PPP-B2b selected as a representative case (B2b-ARTK).

By introducing backward ephemeris error modeling, service, delivering orbit, clock, and differential code bias the method achieves high-frequency real-time position- (DCB) corrections with wide coverage, communication ing with a delay of several seconds, making it suitable for independence, and high accuracy (Xu et al.
